_comment: Line/span- and query-attributed ground truth for the Juice Shop graded corpus (specs/2026-07-04-juice-shop-graded-corpus.md, DECISIONS.md#057/#060/#061). Discriminated row union: kind='expected_finding' asserts a located (query, line) expectation — real_vulnerability=true for a real vulnerability the catalog should reach, real_vulnerability=false for a documented non-vulnerability pinning a tolerated FP; kind='expected_clean' asserts the catalog must emit nothing for a file (optionally scoped to one query). 'current_outcome' on expected_finding rows is what the catalog does TODAY (emitted | denied_at_admission | no_raw_match); when it is not 'emitted' for a real vuln, 'residual_tag' names the deferred mechanism that explains the gap. Every vendored corpus file must carry at least one row (grade() enforces totality) and query_match_id/finding_type are cross-checked against the live registry. The scorecard pins current behavior; a change in either direction (a TP regressing, or a residual closing) makes the structural test fail loudly so ground truth gets revisited.

rows.rationale: crypto.createHash('md5') with `import crypto from 'node:crypto'` — the default value import binds the receiver; member-arm join admits. MD5 for password hashing (Juice Shop's `security.hash`).
eval(expression) on a built string — global eval, unshadowed, inside the captchas() scope; admits.
eval(code) where code derives from the username (SSTI-adjacent RCE) — global eval, unshadowed, inside getUserProfile().

models.sequelize.query(`...${criteria}...`) — a genuine union-based SQLi. The query RAW-matches, but module_presence needs a direct SQL-driver import; Juice Shop reaches the driver via `models.sequelize` (no `import ... from 'sequelize'` in this file), so admission denies. This is the FUP-214 file-level/per-receiver residual, a declared non-goal of this arc — MEASURED and ACCEPTED, not fixed. Closing it needs assignment-flow, not a query edit.
models.sequelize.query(`...${req.body.email}...`) — auth-bypass SQLi. Same residual as search.ts: RAW-matches, denied at admission because the driver is not directly imported in this file.

new Function('specifier', 'return import(specifier)') — all arguments are string literals, so it is constant code, not a dynamic-code sink; the query correctly excludes all-literal `new Function`. A true negative (also module-level, so the scope gate would drop it regardless).

Imports `sequelize` directly (`{ Op }`), so the sql_injection module_presence pre-gate is SATISFIED — yet the file only uses the ORM (no string-concat `.query()` sink), so nothing raw-matches. Positive control that module_presence-satisfied alone never produces a finding without a structural match.

new RegExp(...).exec(...) — RegExp.prototype.exec, not child_process. No child_process import and the receiver is a new-expression, so the command-injection query's binding requirement correctly denies. The exact FP class (an `exec` that is not a shell) the import-binding step closed.
